claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

The first thing I need to know is about the child (or children) you're claiming for.

**How old is your child?**

Please let me know which category applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children you'd like to claim for, please tell me about each of them and their ages.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, hi! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have two children actually - Alex who's 8 and Blake who's 9. So they're both under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful! Thank you for that information. So you have two children:
- **Alex** - 8 years old
- **Blake** - 9 years old

Since both Alex and Blake are under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about your children's work status.

**Are both Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Given that Alex is 8 and Blake is 9, I would expect they're not in paid employment - but I just need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not working at all - they're just in primary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense! At 8 and 9 years old, Alex and Blake are in primary school and not working. That's a "yes" to this check - they're working less than 24 hours per week (in fact, not at all).

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake NOT receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This means they're not personally claiming benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit themselves. At their ages in primary school, this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they don't get any benefits themselves - they're just kids!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly, that's what I'd expect! So both Alex and Blake are not receiving any benefits in their own right. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about apprenticeships:

**Are Alex and Blake in any kind of apprenticeship?**

Given they're 8 and 9 years old and in primary school, I'm confident the answer is no - but just to confirm they're not in any apprenticeship programmes?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not - they're way too young for that!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely right! At 8 and 9 years old, they're definitely not in any apprenticeships. Let's move on to the next check, which will be about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check your residency status. You can qualify through **any one** of the following paths:

**Do you meet any of these residency requirements?**

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You're a UK resident living here
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ months)
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (without claiming certain benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as a family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

The most common situation is simply that you **normally live in the UK**. Does that apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! You normally live in the UK (in Manchester), so you meet the residency requirements. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check your responsibility for Alex and Blake. You can qualify through **any one** of these:

**Are you responsible for Alex and Blake in any of these ways?**

1. **Living with the children** - Alex and Blake live with you
2. **Contributing to their upkeep** - You pay at least the Child Benefit amount towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - You make maintenance payments under a court order or agreement

Do you live with Alex and Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Since Alex and Blake both live with you, you clearly have responsibility for them. Let's continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now an important question about other potential claimants:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?**

For example, is there another parent, guardian, or family member who might be claiming Child Benefit for either of them?

- **No one else is claiming** for them
- **Someone else was claiming, but we've agreed I should claim instead**
- **There's a dispute** about who should claim

Which situation applies to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Since no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake, we can continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about where Alex and Blake are currently living:

**Are Alex and Blake in any special living situations?**

- **In hospital** - Either child is currently staying in hospital
- **In care** - Either child is in local authority care
- **Fostered** - Either child is in a foster care arrangement
- **Living normally at home** - Both children are living at home with you in a normal arrangement

Since you mentioned they live at home with you in Manchester and attend primary school, I assume they're **living normally at home** - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - they're just living at home with me normally.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Both Alex and Blake are living at home with you normally. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're almost there! Just one final check:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex and Blake's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, you're not receiving any local council payments specifically for housing or looking after the children?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything for them - I'm just looking after them myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful! That confirms everything I need to know. Since the local council is not paying towards Alex or Blake's accommodation or maintenance, you meet all the eligibility requirements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
# 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit

Based on the assessment, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake**.

## Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? |
|-------|-----|-----------|
| Alex | 8 | ✅ Yes |
| Blake | 9 | ✅ Yes |

Both children meet all the requirements because:
- They are under 16 years old
- They are not working
- They are not receiving benefits in their own right
- They are not in apprenticeships
- You live in the UK
- They live with you at home
- No one else is claiming for them
- The local council isn't paying for their care

## Next Steps to Claim

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and both children
3. **Provide birth certificates** for Alex and Blake

## Important Information

- **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)
- ⚠️ **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may need to pay some or all of the benefit back through tax. You can still claim but should be aware of this.

## Useful Link
👉 [Apply for Child Benefit on GOV.UK](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 8) qualifies for Child Benefit because: 1) They are under 16 years old, 2) They are not working, 3) They are not receiving benefits in their own right, 4) They are not in an apprenticeship, 5) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), 6) The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for them, 7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, 8) Alex is living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered), and 9) The local council is not paying for Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 9) qualifies for Child Benefit because: 1) They are under 16 years old, 2) They are not working, 3) They are not receiving benefits in their own right, 4) They are not in an apprenticeship, 5) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), 6) The claimant lives with Blake and is responsible for them, 7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, 8) Blake is living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered), and 9) The local council is not paying for Blake's accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children. They are a UK resident living in Manchester with two children under 16 (Alex, 8 and Blake, 9) who live at home with them. Both children are in primary school, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in apprenticeships. No one else is claiming for either child, and the local council is not contributing to their care. All eligibility criteria are met for both children.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate

Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (age 8) qualifies for Child Benefit because: 1) They are under 16 years old, 2) They are not working, 3) They are not receiving benefits in their own right, 4) They are not in an apprenticeship, 5) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), 6) The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for them, 7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, 8) Alex is living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered), and 9) The local council is not paying for Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 9) qualifies for Child Benefit because: 1) They are under 16 years old, 2) They are not working, 3) They are not receiving benefits in their own right, 4) They are not in an apprenticeship, 5) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), 6) The claimant lives with Blake and is responsible for them, 7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, 8) Blake is living normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered), and 9) The local council is not paying for Blake's accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children. They are a UK resident living in Manchester with two children under 16 (Alex, 8 and Blake, 9) who live at home with them. Both children are in primary school, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in apprenticeships. No one else is claiming for either child, and the local council is not contributing to their care. All eligibility criteria are met for both children.
